summary: In desktop-v0.1.0, operator and local inference startup failures could appear as no-ops because sidecar stderr was not surfaced and frontend error handling left ambiguous UI states.
impact: Desktop MVP users could not reliably diagnose startup failures for local smoke tests or operator relay registration.
fix: Drain and prefix-log sidecar stderr/stdout parse errors in Rust host, emit explicit startup/runtime error events, run sidecar lifecycles in background tasks so start invokes return promptly, harden frontend failed-state handling, and add actionable relay compatibility guidance.
lessons: Desktop host process observability must treat child stderr as first-class signal and startup paths must always drive deterministic UI failure states.
rootCause: Rust Tauri sidecar orchestration piped child stderr without draining/logging it and frontend invoke-failure handling did not consistently transition UI status to failure, producing silent startup failures.
symptoms: Start operator appeared to do nothing while Running/Registered stayed no
Start local inference appeared to do nothing and could remain stuck in starting
Errors were easy to lose because child stderr was not visible
